source: Petitions. Gazetted officers of the Malayan Police Service__CO273:660:6
filename: Petitions. Gazetted officers of the Malayan Police Service__CO273:660:6.txt
summary:
- Petition/memorial (file CO 273/660/6) from **gazetted officers of the Malayan Police Service** seeking a **uniform salary scale** and protesting their exclusion from benefits of a **revised probationer salary scheme**.  
- **Geography/administration:** **Straits Settlements and Federated Malay States (Malaya)**; correspondence between **Singapore/Malayan Establishment Office** and the **Colonial Office, Downing Street (London)**.  
- **Time period:** petition dated **22 Feb 1939**; further submissions **3 Jan 1940**; Colonial Office decision communicated **12 Aug 1940** (with internal minutes June–Aug 1940).  
- **Core grievance/proposal:** officers recruited as **Police Probationers 1927–1934** argued that after the **revised scale effective 1 Jan 1937** (HQ Order No. 30 dated **27 Jul 1938**), officers of equal rank were on **three different salary scales**; they sought extension of the revised scale to them to remove a **$50/month** disparity at early stages (e.g., **$250/$300/$350** vs **$300/$350/$400** for probationer/passed/confirmed).  
- **Government decision:** the Colonial Office concluded the revised scheme was intended only to **raise initial probationer salaries** and to adjust pay only so **no junior exceeded a senior**, not to grant a **general enhancement** to all time-scale officers; therefore the **petition was rejected**, explicitly following the precedent of a similar **Malayan Customs Service** petition.  
- **Key actors:** petitioners (list includes **43 named signatories** in the submission to the Secretary of State); **S. W. Jones** (Officer Administering the Government & High Commissioner) forwarded the memorial and argued against concession; **C. H. Sansom** (Inspector-General of Police, F.M.S.) largely opposed the claim; **A. H. Dickinson** (Ag. Inspector-General of Police, Straits Settlements) expressed sympathy and noted support from **R. H. de S. Omraet** (late IGP, S.S.); Colonial Office officials referenced include **Lord Lloyd** (Secretary of State addressed) and internal sign-offs/minutes.  
- **Notable numbers/dates used in arguments:** recruitment cohorts compared (**45 officers, 1927–1931** vs **21 officers, 1934–1937**); petitioners’ computed **average recruitment age 20 years 6½ months** vs new recruits **21 years 3 months** (disputing the committee’s “some two years” claim); later note that entry age was raised in **1938** (candidates must be **over 21 and under 22** on 1 January of selection year).
